title: P0-3: Migration runner missing (schema/migrations.ts absent)
description: plans/phase-1.md line 73 promised packages/db/src/schema/migrations.ts containing applyMigrations(db, fs, logger) plus schema_version tracking, integrity_check, and idempotent version gating. The file does not exist on disk. The schema_version table is created in tables.ts but is never read or written. openDb (connection.ts line 33) just iterates TABLES_DDL on every open, with no version gating. Effect: every open re-runs CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS (cheap but unprincipled), and there is no path to evolve the schema (column add, index change, table rename) in v1.1+. Fix: write schema/migrations.ts exporting applyMigrations. It should (a) read current version from schema_version, (b) iterate the ordered migration list, (c) for each unapplied migration, run inside a tx, (d) write the new version. Running twice must be a no-op. Verify by opening a fresh db, running applyMigrations, asserting version equals latest migration; run again, assert still latest and no duplicate applies.
close_reason: P0-3: Created schema/migrations.ts with applyMigrations, integrated into connection.ts. P1-5: Extended schema/rows.ts with 6 missing row types (Snapshot, Artifact, Edge, ConstraintRule, SchemaVersion, InboxProcessed). P1-6: Added hypotheses table stub to TABLES_DDL and FK on events.linked_hypothesis_id → hypotheses(id). 41/41 tests green.

title: P0-2: Redaction audit gap — redaction_applied events never emitted
description: redactEvent (redaction.ts line 99) calls scanValue with the payload as the value and path defaulted to empty string. scanValue's string branch returns hits with fieldPath set to the path argument, so every hit from a root-level scan gets fieldPath equal to empty string. The appendEvent loop at event-store.ts line 232-234 then filters out those hits. Net effect: in the normal append flow, redaction_applied audit rows are NEVER written. The secret IS masked in payload_json, but no audit trail exists. The plan explicitly required an audit row per hit. Fix: thread the path properly. Either (a) redactEvent scans a wrapper object that exposes the event type as the root key, or (b) remove the fieldPath-empty filter in event-store.ts and assign a non-empty path like the type name for top-level string hits. Verify by appending an event with a JWT or AWS key in a nested field, querying events for the session, and asserting at least one redaction_applied row exists with the correct field_path.
close_reason: redactEvent now wraps payload/source in {value: ...} envelope before scanValue, ensuring all hits have non-empty fieldPath (payload.* or source.*). Updated RedactorShape.scanValue to accept optional path arg. Added 4 new tests: 3 in redaction.test.ts (top-level string, nested object, array-indexed) + 1 in event-store.test.ts (verifying field_path starts with payload.). All 41 db tests pass, tsc clean.

title: P0-1: Inbox forked fiber missing R channel (runtime crash)
description: inbox.ts line 179: the chokidar 'add' handler runs the processFile Effect via Effect.runFork, with an unsafe cast to Effect<void, never, never> that strips the EventStore and Logger R-channel. processFile needs EventStore and Logger to do its work. At runtime the forked fiber has no EventStore/Logger in context, so it dies with MissingServiceError the first time it tries to yield either service. Chokidar swallows the fiber death, so the operator sees nothing. Production-breaking: the inbox watcher cannot append any events. Fix options: build a ManagedRuntime from the caller's services (Layer.toRuntime) and call runtime.runFork, or use a Queue + worker fiber so the R-channel is carried into the consumer. Verify by smoke-running the watcher against a temp dir and dropping a real .json file: assert DB has a new event row.
close_reason: Closed

title: P1-8: appendEvent flow deviates from plan (order + actor_registered skip)
description: plans/phase-1.md line 47-56 specifies 9-step flow order: type known → ensureActor → session exists → idempotency → redact → redaction_applied loop → insert event → return. event-store.ts:144-267 implements: type known → validate → idempotency → session exists → tx{ensureActor → redact → insert event → redaction_applied loop → return}. Deviations: (a) ensureActor moved inside tx (plan: before). (b) validation added (plan: implicit). (c) redaction_applied emitted after main event (plan: before). (d) actor_registered is skipped from validation even though ActorRegisteredPayload schema exists. Also: nowIso() called twice per append (line 213 and 256) so redaction_applied timestamps can be after main event. Decision: either revert to plan order exactly (clean spec match, but ensureActor in plan was outside the tx and would not be atomic with the event insert — arguably a worse design), or keep impl order and amend the plan. Recommendation: keep impl order (atomicity is more important than step numbering), amend plan.xml to match. Drop the actor_registered validation skip (validate it). Capture nowIso once at the top of the tx and reuse for both inserts.
close_reason: Closed

title: P1-7: inbox actor_type unvalidated; chokidar ignored is substring match
description: Two inbox issues from the audit: (1) inbox.ts line 116: 'const actorType = p.actor_type as ActorType' is an unchecked cast. Bad values reach the DB and are rejected late by the CHECK constraint on actors.type, after the JSON parse already passed. The error message that surfaces to the operator is a SQLite CHECK-constraint violation, not a clean 'invalid actor_type'. Fix: validate p.actor_type against the literal tuple ['human','worker','system'] (or use Schema.decode) before passing to append; on failure, move file to _error/ with a clear log message. (2) inbox.ts line 170: chokidar's ignored function uses substring match ('_error' or 'processed'). A file named 'my_processed_backup.json' would be silently skipped. Fix: change to path-prefix match using path.sep + '_error' or 'processed' as a path segment.
close_reason: Closed

title: P1-6: events.linked_hypothesis_id missing FK to hypotheses(id)
description: tables.ts line 54: events.linked_hypothesis_id is declared as plain TEXT with no REFERENCES clause. plan.xml line 314 specifies 'REFERENCES hypotheses(id)'. The hypotheses table is not in the DDL yet (it's a planned future table), so the FK cannot be added now without first creating the hypotheses table. Fix: add a CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS hypotheses (...) stub to TABLES_DDL (matching plan.xml hypotheses schema), then add the FK on events.linked_hypothesis_id. Verify with PRAGMA foreign_key_list(events) returning the expected row. Alternative: add the FK as a deferred migration in schema/migrations.ts (Fix C) so v1.0.0 ships without hypotheses and v1.0.1 adds both the table and the FK atomically.
close_reason: P0-3: Created schema/migrations.ts with applyMigrations, integrated into connection.ts. P1-5: Extended schema/rows.ts with 6 missing row types (Snapshot, Artifact, Edge, ConstraintRule, SchemaVersion, InboxProcessed). P1-6: Added hypotheses table stub to TABLES_DDL and FK on events.linked_hypothesis_id → hypotheses(id). 41/41 tests green.

title: P1-5: schema/rows.ts incomplete (4/10 tables, created_at type drift)
description: schema/rows.ts declares only ProjectRow, SessionRow, ActorRow, EventRow (4 of 10 tables). Missing: SnapshotRow, ArtifactRow, EdgeRow, ConstraintRuleRow, SchemaVersionRow, InboxProcessedRow. Also: EventRow.created_at is typed as 'string' but plans/phase-1.md line 137 declares it 'Date' — the comment 'Row shapes (mirror DDL exactly)' is technically true for SQLite (TEXT) but the spec is Date. Fix: add the 6 missing row types, mirror the DDL exactly, and decide on the created_at convention: either change all created_at to Date and add a parsing layer at the SqliteHandle boundary, or keep them as string and update the plan + interfaces. Recommendation: keep string (matches DDL + the SqliteHandle returns raw TEXT); update plan.md and any consumer interfaces that assumed Date.
close_reason: P0-3: Created schema/migrations.ts with applyMigrations, integrated into connection.ts. P1-5: Extended schema/rows.ts with 6 missing row types (Snapshot, Artifact, Edge, ConstraintRule, SchemaVersion, InboxProcessed). P1-6: Added hypotheses table stub to TABLES_DDL and FK on events.linked_hypothesis_id → hypotheses(id). 41/41 tests green.

title: P1-4: EventValidator service is dead code + silently broken
description: event-schema.ts line 204-221: EventValidatorLive.validate silently returns the original payload on validation failure, unknown type, or non-current version. The Tag is declared, the Live layer is exported and included in leafs (live.ts:28), but no consumer in the live flow yields* EventValidator — event-store.ts and migrate.ts both inline Schema.decodeUnknownEither against PAYLOAD_SCHEMAS_V1 directly. Decision needed: (a) delete the EventValidator Tag and its Live layer entirely (it serves no purpose), or (b) fix validate to actually return Either/Option so the API contract is sound, and rewire appendEvent and migratePayload to consume it. The plan (phase-1.md:39) listed EventStore as depending on EventValidator — option (b) would honor the plan. Recommendation: option (a) delete it, then update plan.xml to remove the EventValidator row. Lower surface area, less indirection.
close_reason: Closed

title: P1-3: Idempotency race — concurrent duplicate id can throw raw unique-constraint
description: event-store.ts line 172-181: idempotency check is 'SELECT then INSERT' outside the tx. Two concurrent appends with the same id can both miss the SELECT, both enter the tx, both attempt INSERT, and the second hits a UNIQUE constraint violation (events.id is PRIMARY KEY). better-sqlite3 throws this synchronously, not as Effect.fail. Net effect: a second caller of append with the same id can either get back the existing row (if it lost the race) or get an unhandled exception (if it won the INSERT but the other committed first). Fix options: (a) catch UNIQUE constraint inside the tx and re-fetch the existing row, returning it; (b) move the idempotency check INSIDE the tx, then INSERT — the second tx will see the first tx's row and can re-fetch on UNIQUE; (c) use INSERT ... ON CONFLICT(id) DO NOTHING + re-fetch. Prefer (b) for cleanest semantics. Update tests with a concurrent-append test.
close_reason: Closed

title: P1-2: Synchronous throws escape Effect error channel (tx rollback won't fire)
description: event-store.ts: ensureActor (line 71, 79), insertEvent (line 92), fetchEvent (line 124) all call conn.handle.run/get directly, which can throw raw better-sqlite3 errors (driver error, FK violation, unique constraint). These are NOT wrapped in Effect.try, so they escape the Effect error channel as defects. The tx wrapper at connection.ts:62 only rolls back on Effect.fail via tapError — synchronous throws from inside the tx will not trigger ROLLBACK and may leave the connection in a half-state. Fix: wrap every handle.run/get call inside event-store, ensureActor, insertEvent, fetchEvent in Effect.try with onError mapping to DbError.
close_reason: Closed

title: P1-1: event-store get() throws NotFound as defect, not Effect.fail
description: event-store.ts line 296-303: get() does 'throw new NotFound(...)' inside Effect.sync. Effect.sync wraps a synchronous throw as a defect, not a typed failure. The signature in context.ts says 'Effect<EventRow, never>' — never allows Effect.fail(NotFound) at compile time. Acceptance criterion #9 ('get returns row or throws NotFound via Effect.fail') is not met. Fix: wrap the lookup in Effect.try (or use Either) so a missing row becomes a typed NotFound failure, and the get signature should be 'Effect.Effect<EventRow, NotFound>'. Update callers/tests that depend on throw semantics.
close_reason: Closed

title: 1h. Tests: event-store round-trip, redaction hits, migration v0.x->1.0.0, idempotency
description: vitest. (1) append + list round-trip preserves payload. (2) append with JWT in payload -> redacted, redaction_applied event present, original token NOT in db. (3) fixture v0.0.1 events migrate to 1.0.0 cleanly. (4) duplicate appendEvent(id=X) returns existing row, no double insert. (5) WAL integrity_check passes on temp db.
close_reason: Phase 1 implementation complete: all 7 P1 deliverables shipped across commits 693848f, 273309d, b4eb156, 1d88ef7. 46/46 tests pass, tsc clean. Bead breakdown: 1a scaffold (packages/db with Effect Layer arch) → 1b drizzle schema + migration runner (TABLES_DDL + applyMigrations + WAL + integrity_check) → 1c Effect Schema registry (PAYLOAD_SCHEMAS_V1 + CURRENT_VERSION) → 1d migration utility (migratePayload + semver) → 1e redaction service (Redactor Tag + Live + redactEvent envelope) → 1f EventStore service (append + list + get with NotFound failure) → 1h tests (46 cases covering all acceptance criteria)

title: 1f. EventStore Service: appendEvent, listEvents, getEvent (Effect methods)

title: 2k. Phase 2 docs + commit + push
description: docs/phase-2-results.md (one-screen summary: what shipped, what was deferred, how to verify). Update plan.xml if a section materially changed. Update ARCHITECTURE.md's read paths section to mention the reducer. git add + commit + push.

title: 2j. End-to-end: snapshot+tail replay + resume-as-fork
description: packages/db/test/reducer-integration.test.ts: end-to-end the done_when. Insert 100+ events, take snapshot, append tail, call SessionService.show, assert state matches. packages/cli/test/session.test.ts: spawn the CLI, run session create + append events + session close + session show, assert stdout. Resume-as-fork test: assert new session row + new session_created event with parent_session_id.

title: 2i. CLI: cognit session {create,list,resume,pause,close,show} + cognit snapshot
description: packages/cli/src/commands/session.ts: registerSession(program) wires cognit session create/list/resume/pause/close/show. packages/cli/src/commands/snapshot.ts: registerSnapshot(program) wires cognit snapshot [--session id]. packages/cli/src/layer-build.ts: shared Layer<DbConnection | EventStore | SessionService | SnapshotService | Logger> builder from a project root. Use --root to override (default: process.cwd()). All commands print human-readable output (no JSON by default in phase 2).

title: 2g. Snapshot policy integration: explicit + on-close
description: Wire SnapshotService into: (1) SessionService.close -- take a snapshot inside the same tx that flips status to closed. (2) explicit trigger (CLI in 2i). Auto-N-event trigger is deferred (no background watcher in phase 2; manual path + on-close are the only ship-blocking integrations).
close_reason: Closed

title: 2f. SnapshotService: write / latestForSession / takeIfDue
description: packages/db/src/snapshot-service.ts: SnapshotService Tag + Live. write({sessionId, state, eventId, eventCount}) inserts snapshots row and updates sessions.last_snapshot_event_id. latestForSession(sessionId) returns the most recent snapshot row (or undefined). takeIfDue({sessionId, currentEventCount, everyN}) writes a snapshot if (currentEventCount - lastEventCount) >= everyN. Pure-function reducer dependency is injected from core (no core->db import direction violation).
close_reason: Closed

title: 2e. SessionService.resume (fork or reopen) + session show reducer view
description: packages/db/src/session-service.ts (edit): resume({idOrGoal, fork}, actor) -- when fork=true (default), INSERT new session with parent_session_id, emit session_created with parent_session_id in payload. When fork=false, reopen by emitting a new session_created linking to the parent only via events (do not create a new row). show(sessionId, actor) returns SessionState via reducer (load snapshot + tail). Add UnknownSessionForResume error.
close_reason: Closed

title: 2d. SessionService tests
description: packages/db/test/session-service.test.ts: create a session, list it, get by id and by goal substring, pause + close. Edge cases: ambiguous goal (multiple open sessions with same goal) returns most recent + warning, unknown id returns typed error, closing an already-closed session is a no-op + warning.
close_reason: Closed

title: 2c. SessionService: create / list / getByGoalOrId / pause / close
description: packages/db/src/session-service.ts: SessionService Tag + Live. create({projectId, goal, parentSessionId?}, actor) emits session_created event. list({status?}). getByGoalOrId({id?, goal?, preferMostRecent?}). pause(sessionId, actor) emits session_paused. close(sessionId, actor) emits session_closed. Uses DbConnection. No fork logic in this bead.
close_reason: Closed

title: 2b. Reducer unit tests in core
description: packages/core/test/reducer.test.ts: every event type (hypothesis_created/weakened/rejected/promoted, decision_*/4 states, conclusion_*3, verification_started/passed/failed/errored/cancelled/rerun, experiment_created/completed, theory_*4, session_*3, observation_recorded, finding_created, artifact_attached, edge_created). Assert non-state events do not change state. Snapshot restore + tail replay. Idempotency: re-folding the same event list yields the same state.
close_reason: Closed

title: 2a. Reducer state types + pure reduce() in core
description: packages/core/src/state.ts: SessionState + entity-state types (HypothesisState, DecisionState, ConclusionState, VerificationState, ...). packages/core/src/reducer.ts: pure reduce(events: ReadonlyArray<EventRow>, snapshot?: SessionState): SessionState. Total fold over (created_at, id) order. Skip non-state events (project_created, actor_registered, redaction_applied, constraint_rule_*, snapshot_created). Tests: every event type handled, snapshot restore + tail replay.

Tests cover rename/move/duplicate.

All in single tx. duplicate id returns existing. unknown session/typed rejected with tagged errors. Depends on DbConnection/Redactor/Uuid/Logger.

Need both for phase 2 manual + automated testing.

title: 1g. Inbox adapter: atomic rename ingest -> appendEvent
description: packages/db/src/inbox.ts. Watches .cognit/inbox/*.json (chokidar). On rename to .json: parse, validate, appendEvent, then move to .cognit/processed/<id>.json (or .error/). Per-file Promise chain to avoid concurrent write. Idempotent: if event.id already in events table, skip.
close_reason: Work done via Cognit-2k5 (inbox R-channel) + Cognit-eeg (chokidar path-segment + actor_type validation). Atomic rename ingest, race-safety, idempotency all implemented in packages/db/src/inbox.ts. Test coverage: 6 tests in inbox.test.ts including 2 new tests for the watcher smoke + path-segment match.
